data.description:
## Problem
prerequisites.py run_focused_finder emits emit_indeterminate(model=cfg.model) at line ~235, but run_bin escalates up a model ladder (models_at_or_above) on context-limit errors and re-runs on next_model. When escalation fires, the record was produced by the escalated model while the log reports cfg.model — e.g. logs 'claude-haiku-4-5' while the payload ran on 'claude-sonnet-4-6'. attempt_count is also hardcoded 1 at the call site, conveying nothing about retries.

## Root cause (confirmed by reading)
run_bin(bin_, model) returns records but does NOT carry the model that produced them; the outer loop reports cfg.model unconditionally. src/rebar/llm/plan_review/prerequisites.py:189-238.

## Fix
Thread the actual model that produced each record through to emit_indeterminate. Either return (records, model_used) from run_bin per record, or stamp the producing model onto each indeterminate record and read it back. Wire attempt_count to the real retry depth or drop the field.

## Acceptance criteria
- When run_bin escalates to a higher model, the emitted plan_review_prerequisite_indeterminate log 'model' field equals the escalated model, not cfg.model.
- A unit test drives an escalation (context-limit error forces next_model) and asserts the logged/emitted model equals the escalated model; asserting cfg.model would FAIL.
- attempt_count reflects real attempts or is removed (test asserts either the wired value or absence).

## Verification
pytest the new test; assert emitted model == escalated model under a forced escalation.
data.title: plan-review indeterminate log reports configured model, not the model that actually ran
